channel.title: Policy & Security Think Tank Updates
channel.description: Custom RSS feed from CSIS, CFR, and other policy institutions

item.title: International Collaboration Took the United States Back to the Moon
item.description: Artemis II was more than a U.S. mission—it was a global effort. The success of U.S. lunar exploration depends on sustaining international partnerships that provide essential capabilities, legitimacy, and long-term leadership in space.

item.title: What Comes Next for Artemis?
item.description: With the completion of Artemis II, NASA sets its sights on its next Moon mission. To get there, NASA has rolled out a new plan for Artemis. This new plan addresses risks for the old plan, leaves some risks unchanged, and introduces new risks.

item.title: The Case for a Sustained U.S. Presence on the Moon
item.description: Returning to the Moon is essential for the future; the United States must lead and be present to assure a democratic future.

item.title: A New Generation Looks to the Moon: Perspectives on Artemis
item.description: For a generation born after the Apollo 17 mission in 1972, it represents not just a technical milestone, but a defining moment—a generation's first lived experience of the United States' renewed push into deep space.
